metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the smoke test as a concrete, runnable command with a defined pass condition (e.g. `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version` outputs `0.7.1`), and add it explicitly to the AC.
findings.finding: The 'post-release smoke test' AC and step are entirely unspecified: no command, no artifact, no observable pass/fail criterion is given, leaving an executing agent to decide what the test is.
findings.location: Scope/steps ('Post-release smoke test against the published artifact') and AC ('Post-release smoke test passes against the published artifact')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Post-release smoke test passes against the published artifact' with: 'Post-release smoke test passes: `pip install --no-cache-dir nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version` prints `0.7.1` (or equivalent MCP server start/stop check if the primary artifact is the server).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a post-release step to update `docs/releasing.md` to reflect that `github_release` and `mcp_registry` jobs are production-verified and fully automated, removing or archiving any manual-fallback language dating from the v0.7.0 era.
findings.finding: docs/releasing.md may still contain manual-step language or provisional notes from the v0.7.0 era (when the GitHub Release was a manual one-off), yet the plan includes no post-release step to update it, potentially stranding stale references once the automation is proven.
findings.location: Scope / steps section — 'Land the "Release 0.7.1" bump commit' / 'Tag v0.7.1 and push'; no post-release doc-update step present
findings.suggested_fix: After confirming all AC pass, open a follow-up commit (or amend the release commit) that removes/updates any 'manually create GitHub Release' or 'manual MCP Registry login' notes in docs/releasing.md and adds a line such as: 'As of v0.7.1, both github_release and mcp_registry steps are fully automated via the release.yml workflow.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a partial-failure recovery section: specify whether each job (github_release, mcp_registry) is safe to re-run idempotently (e.g., does re-running github_release on an already-created release fail or update it?), and document the operator steps for each failure scenario.
findings.finding: The plan has no recovery/rollback specification for partial pipeline failure: if PyPI publish succeeds but the downstream `github_release` or `mcp_registry` job fails, there is no stated procedure to resume or repair without creating a duplicate or inconsistent release state.
findings.location: Scope / steps — tag push → release.yml pipeline; Acceptance Criteria lines for GitHub Release and MCP Registry
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/releasing.md, add a 'Failure recovery' table: for each job (build, publish, github_release, mcp_registry), state (a) whether re-running the workflow after fixing the root cause is safe/idempotent, (b) any manual cleanup needed (e.g., delete the draft release before re-run if the GH API errors on duplicate), and (c) how to verify state before re-running.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how operators will be notified if `github_release` or `mcp_registry` jobs fail (e.g., GitHub Actions email/Slack notification, required status check on the release workflow, or a post-release verification step that checks the live endpoints).
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the two new automated jobs (`github_release`, `mcp_registry`): there is no mention of workflow failure notifications, status badges, or alerts, so a silent job failure may go undetected.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'github_release (auto GitHub Release) → mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document or verify that `github_release` and `mcp_registry` jobs are idempotent under re-run (e.g., github_release uses upsert semantics or checks for existence before creating; mcp_registry publish is a no-op for an already-published identical version).
findings.finding: Workflow re-run idempotency for the new `github_release` and `mcp_registry` jobs is not addressed; if the tag is re-pushed or the workflow is manually re-run after a first-run failure (likely given this is their debut), it is unclear whether these jobs will safely no-op or produce duplicate/conflicting state.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'Tag v0.7.1 and push the tag'; Acceptance Criteria — github_release and mcp_registry ACs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `mcp_registry` GitHub Actions OIDC job referenced as 'change 319' actually exists in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (or another workflow file) before tagging v0.7.1; if it does not exist, either create and merge it first or revise the plan and AC to reflect that MCP Registry publishing for this release is still manual.
findings.finding: The plan's central premise — that 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json' already exists — is unverifiable in the codebase. No mcp_registry job exists anywhere in .github/workflows/. release.yml ends at the github_release job (line 107); no workflow file contains 'mcp_registry', 'mcp-publisher', or any OIDC-based MCP Registry automation. docs/releasing.md §4 still describes manual interactive login (mcp-publisher login github, browser/device-flow), not an automated OIDC job.
findings.location: Plan § What / Scope / steps / AC — 'mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the mcp_registry job to release.yml (after github_release, same tag-push gate) that installs mcp-publisher, uses id-token: write to obtain an OIDC token, and calls mcp-publisher publish with that token. Only after that job lands and is verified via workflow_dispatch dry-run should the v0.7.1 tag be pushed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `mcp_registry` workflow job from 'change 319' has actually been merged to main (it is absent from `.github/workflows/release.yml`); if not yet landed, the plan must either land it before tagging v0.7.1 or remove the OIDC-automation AC and describe the MCP Registry step as still manual.
findings.finding: The plan names `mcp_registry` as an already-landed workflow job in `release.yml` (from 'change 319') that auto-publishes `server.json` via GitHub Actions OIDC. This job does not exist in `.github/workflows/release.yml` — the file contains only `build`, `publish`, and `github_release`. The AC 'mcp_registry OIDC job' therefore references a hallucinated/not-yet-landed edit target.
findings.location: Scope/steps section ('the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'); AC line 'The MCP Registry serves io.github.navapbc/rebar 0.7.1, published automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job'; `.github/workflows/release.yml`
findings.suggested_fix: Before pushing the v0.7.1 tag, confirm `mcp_registry` appears as a job in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (e.g. `grep -n 'mcp_registry' .github/workflows/release.yml`). If absent, land change 319 first, then re-verify the workflow, and update `docs/releasing.md` §4 to reflect the now-automated step before cutting the release.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm change 319 (the `mcp_registry` OIDC job) is actually merged to `release.yml` before pushing the v0.7.1 tag; if not, either land it first (the job must appear in `release.yml` with `needs: publish`, `id-token: write`, and the `mcp-publisher` OIDC auth step) or revise the plan to acknowledge that the MCP Registry step remains manual for this release.
findings.finding: The plan's central mechanism — the `mcp_registry` GitHub Actions OIDC job ('change 319') that auto-publishes `server.json` — does not exist in the repo. `release.yml` contains only three jobs (`build`, `publish`, `github_release`); there is no `mcp_registry` job in any workflow file. Pushing the `v0.7.1` tag will NOT trigger automated MCP Registry publishing; the MCP Registry channel will silently fall through to the manual device-flow in `docs/releasing.md` §4 — exactly what v0.7.0 used — making the plan's stated goal of a 'fully hands-off' MCP Registry publish impossible to achieve.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC)' bullet; AC line '…mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the `mcp_registry` job to `release.yml` (after `needs: publish`, gated to `push` + `refs/tags/v*`, with `id-token: write` permission and the appropriate `mcp-publisher` OIDC auth + publish steps), then re-verify the job appears in the workflow file before cutting v0.7.1. If the OIDC auth subcommand for `mcp-publisher` is not yet available, document the remaining manual step and remove the OIDC AC until the tooling supports it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `mcp-publisher` ships a non-interactive OIDC/GitHub-Actions auth mode (e.g., `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` or equivalent flag), confirm it supports the `io.github.navapbc/*` org namespace claim without an interactive session, and ensure change 319's `mcp_registry` job is actually committed to `release.yml` before pushing the v0.7.1 tag.
findings.finding: The plan's central novelty — the `mcp_registry` job using 'GitHub Actions OIDC' to publish to the MCP Registry without interactive login — is an unverified capability assertion. The `mcp_registry` job does not exist in the current `release.yml` (the file ends at line 107 with only `build`, `publish`, and `github_release` jobs), and no file in the repo documents a headless/OIDC auth subcommand for `mcp-publisher`. Every codebase reference to `mcp-publisher` auth (docs/releasing.md §Prerequisites line 27, §4 lines 133–138) describes only `mcp-publisher login github` — an interactive browser/device flow that cannot run unattended in CI.
findings.location: Scope/steps — 'mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'; AC line: 'published automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)'
findings.suggested_fix: Before cutting the tag: (1) check the mcp-publisher changelog/docs for a headless GitHub Actions OIDC auth path; (2) if it exists, add the mcp_registry job to release.yml and test it with a workflow_dispatch dry-run (the publish job already has `id-token: write`); (3) if no headless path exists, keep §4 as a manual post-tag step and remove the OIDC AC claim, or open a feature request with the MCP Registry team and defer automation to a future release.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that change 319 (the `mcp_registry` job) is actually merged to main and present in `.github/workflows/release.yml` before tagging v0.7.1; if it is not yet merged, the plan should sequence the tag push *after* change 319 lands, or document a manual fallback for this release.
findings.finding: The plan's central premise — that the `mcp_registry` job (GitHub Actions OIDC; 'change 319') auto-publishing server.json is already landed — is contradicted by the codebase. The actual `release.yml` contains exactly three jobs: `build`, `publish`, `github_release`. No `mcp_registry` job exists anywhere in `.github/workflows/`. The AC requires 'published automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job' but that job does not exist, making the AC unfulfillable as written.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json'; AC: 'published automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit prerequisite gate to the plan: 'Verify `mcp_registry` job is present in `release.yml` on main (`grep mcp_registry .github/workflows/release.yml`) before pushing the v0.7.1 tag. If absent, complete and merge change 319 first.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `mcp-publisher` supports a non-interactive (OIDC or token) auth path suitable for CI before committing to the `mcp_registry` job design; if unconfirmed, add a SPIKE to prototype the auth mechanism or document a manual fallback.
findings.finding: The `mcp_registry` job's OIDC auth mechanism — using a GitHub Actions OIDC token to authenticate to the MCP Registry without interactive login — is an unverified capability. Every codebase reference to mcp-publisher auth is `mcp-publisher login github` (interactive browser/device flow). No `github-oidc` subcommand, CI token exchange path, or equivalent headless auth mechanism for `mcp-publisher` appears anywhere in the repo, docs, or workflow files. The MCP Registry's support for GitHub Actions OIDC as an auth method has not been confirmed.
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'; AC: 'automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job (no interactive login)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the mcp_registry workflow job, pin mcp-publisher to a specific released version and verify its checksum (or install via a pinned pip/uv ref) before executing it on a runner that holds id-token: write.
findings.finding: The plan's mcp_registry CI job (which holds id-token: write to authenticate against the MCP Registry) implicitly fetches and executes the mcp-publisher binary, but the plan specifies no version pin, checksum, or signature verification for that binary. A compromised or tampered mcp-publisher release artifact on the boundary-crossing runner could exfiltrate the short-lived OIDC token and publish arbitrary content to the public MCP Registry under io.github.navapbc/*.
findings.location: Plan section 'Scope / steps' (mcp_registry job) and AC line: 'The MCP Registry serves io.github.navapbc/rebar 0.7.1, published automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job'
findings.suggested_fix: In the mcp_registry job step, install mcp-publisher with an explicit version pin and verify integrity before use, e.g.: `pip install mcp-publisher==<exact-version>` (hash-verified via a lockfile or `--require-hashes`) rather than a curl-fetched binary; or if a binary is required, pin the release tag and verify the published SHA256 checksum against the binary before executing.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
